videos.title: Norway’s Hedegart Claims Victory at 56th Engadin Skimarathon | APT
videos.description:
Einar Hedegart won the men’s race at the Engadin Skimarathon after a tight sprint finish in Switzerland. Hedegart edged Jason Ruesch and Sabin Coupat to claim victory. In the women’s race, 18-year-old Anna Endress beat Swiss favourite Giuliana Werro in a dramatic finish, with Clemence Didierlaurent taking third place. Thousands of skiers competed in the popular 42-km race through the scenic Engadin valley.
#EngadinSkimarathon #CrossCountrySkiing #WinterSports #SportsNews #skirace

videos.title: Short Film: Engadin Skimarathon, by World of Freesports (english version)
videos.description: Emotions, peak performances, stories, and history — that is the legendary Engadin Skimarathon, held every year on the second Sunday in March between Maloja and S-chanf in the Engadin Valley.
videos.transcript:
Every year in early March, thousands of
cross-country skiers from around the
world descend on Engodine, one of
Switzerland's most picturesque high
alpine valleys to take part in the
Enadin Ski Marathon, one of the biggest
sporting events in Switzerland. The
Enodine Ski Marathon has been around
since 1969.
It's been a success story right from the
start and this year we have almost
13,000 participants that have
registered. They come from over 60
different countries, so pretty much from
all over the world to take part in the
world's second biggest cross-country
skiing event.
The Engin Ski Marathon covers 42
kilometers through all of the
communities here. I think it's the one
event where we can really present
ourselves and show how sensational our
high valley is,
but the Enadine is a yearround
destination. In winter, we have
sensational skiing and
snowboarding. What makes our valley
stand out though are the lakes where you
can enjoy sailing, surfing, kite
surfing, and all sorts of other sports.
In cross-country skiing, the Engin Ski
Marathon is together with the Volsa
Love, the equivalent to the big marathon
races in running like the Berlin or New
York marathons. On one hand, it's the
scenery that makes it so nice, but also
the topography of the course, which
isn't too hard and also enables
cross-country skiers who aren't so well
trained to race here. At the same time
though, you're on the start line with
Olympic and world champions. I think the
combination of elite sport, adventure,
and scenery is what makes the Engin Ski
Marathon so
special with four-time Olympic champion
and four-time winner of the Enadin Ski
Marathon, Dario Kona. We of course have
a real celebrity starting here. We have
Justina Kavalic, a double Olympic and
world champion. And in the women's race,
of course, there's Theres Yohag, who
really is the crosscountry skiing
superstar. But we also have a lot of
really good upand cominging athletes. We
have some from Switzerland from the
Swiss ski men's team and some very
strong French skiers. And it's always a
good mix of the top stars and the
amateur athletes.
[Music]
The first race of the Enadine Ski
Marathon Week is the women's race where
the field will tackle a 17 km scenic
course. Before the start in Samadan, all
eyes are on four-time Olympic champion
Theres Yohag of Norway who's competing
for the first time in 2 years after
maternity leave. I think I will be
really nervous because it's two years
since I have competing. Erh, I think
maybe you know the head think that you
are in the same shape as you was for 2
years ago. Amongst the hundreds of women
are some who also use the Enadim women's
race to raise awareness and money for
charity. I raised for Masharosa which is
pink ribbon and it's about breast cancer
prevention and the pink bear was kind of
an obvious costume. There are only women
doing this race and I think it's going
to be a really cool atmosphere. It'll be
fun to take part.
As the start gun goes, almost a thousand
female skiers set off for the first race
of the Enadin Ski Marathon
Week. Right from the start, the
Norwegian superstar Theresio Hog sets
the pace. And despite having a 2-year
hiatus from competition, the 14time
world champion is in a league of her
own.
In the Chasing Pack, Eustina Kavalchic
of Poland and Switzerland's bialon
Olympic silver medalist Selena Gasperin
show their cross-country credentials and
they give it their all to try and keep
the distance to Yohag as small as
possible. Whilst Terresa Yohag is racing
on her own at the front of the pack,
there are hundreds of women behind her
who are enjoying their time together on
the course during the 23rd Engin's
race. They soak up the crowd support and
the stunning Enodine landscape.
It's a joyful occasion and for most of
these cross country enthusiasts, the fun
is more important than the race times
and podium
positions. Back at the front, Theresa
Yohag still dominates the race with the
same group of seven women in pursuit.
Amongst them, Switzerland's bialon star
and engin native Selena
Gasperin. After a dominant race, 14time
world and four-time Olympic champion Yoh
crosses the finish line in Esamp with a
huge gap on the rest of the field and
clearly enjoying her comeback. It was
really fun to have the racing beep on
again and uh have the feeling when you
push yourself in the tracks and uh
people are cheering for me out there. So
it was uh fantastic feeling again. In
second place another former Olympic
champion, Yina Kavalce of
Poland and also happy with her race but
more so with the return to her native
Engadine by athlete Selena Gasperin.
Yes, of course. It's my home. I know
everything here. I've trained here a lot
and I would not have just run any old
race. So, yeah, this is a real
homecoming.
Due to heavy snowfall followed by warm
temperatures, both the Silsa and Silver
Planet lakes can't be prepared in time
for the 2024 Enadina race. So this
year's start is moved to Silver Planner,
another logistical challenge that Menuri
Casper and his team have to deal with.
Yes, it's big and a very complex
undertaking. We have around a thousand
volunteers that work here and all of
them need information on when and where
they need to be places. We have aid
stations on the course and we need
around 20,000 L of warm water for the
participants on race morning. Then we
have to organize the transport for all
of the participants to the start line.
We have more than 64 buses that we've
collected from all over Switzerland and
they're just to drive the people to the
start. We also have more than 40
military trucks that transport the
participants clothes from the start to
the finish.
As the big race day approaches, more and
more competitors arrive in the marathon
village in Pontracina to pick up their
start numbers for the 54th edition of
the Enadinski
Marathon.
Among the thousands of amateur skiers
from around the world is British life
coach Poppy Cross, who's taking part in
the Angadine Ski Marathon for the very
first
time. A friend of mine actually did it a
couple of years ago and said it was one
of the best things he had ever done and
I love the mountains. I love a
challenge. So I thought why not? I've
done downhill for years and years and I
thought the skating technique for the
cross country skiing it wouldn't be too
hard but turns out it's quite technical.
It's quite a challenge but I'm excited.
Swiss friends Michael Akaman and Sirill
Boliga from Burn are equally excited to
take part in the Enadina and for them
this legendary race encapsulates
everything they like about crosscountry
skiing.
You go to places where you normally
wouldn't go. You can enjoy nature. You
can tire yourself out. And at the end of
the day, when you get home, you're
knackered. But it's a healthy tiredness,
and it's the best thing you can do for
your
body. The two friends clearly enjoy the
offerings in the Marathon Village, and
they're happy that the modern
state-of-the-art character of
cross-country skiing is reflected here
in Pontracina.
Yeah, cross country skiing is sometimes
looked upon as boring and old-fashioned,
but here you don't feel that at all. It
really is a cool sport.
Also back in the marathon village is
Frantois Style, aka Madame Enadin Ski
Marathon. She is the only woman to have
completed all Enadina races and clearly
remembers her first one 55 years ago. I
had good stamina but no technique. But I
still made it to the finish without any
problems and I got third in the women's
race.
It went so much better in my second
marathon. And in 1972 I got second in
the Enadinsky Marathon. Marathon. Every
year the 86year-old has got a stall in
the marathon village where she sells her
book Danglau vadam engadinski marathon.
It covers 50 Enadinsky marathons and of
course it's also an autobiography.
There were just so many great marathons
with blue sky and nice warm weather.
Once someone asked me, "What are you
going to wear on your head for
tomorrow's Enadin Ski Marathon?" And I
just said,
hairspray. It was just so nice.
It's about the fascination of the
Enadinsky marathon. The marathon has
always been part of my life. And in
winter, you're just prepared for the
marathon. I mean, every time it's an
adventure, and it's particularly nice if
you can ski on the lakes. It's such
terrific scenery and it's something
special to be one small ant among
thousands of other cross-country skiers.
The second race highlight of the week is
the sixth Enadin night race where skiers
from around the world can experience a
unique and magical adventure on the
moonlit cross country trails in and
around Sills and Pontracena.
You get to meet uh people from all many
different countries. Yeah, that's one of
the really special things about it and
just the excitement around it. We agreed
that he's going to stay with me and I'm
glad about that. It's going to be so
surreal like going through the night
with a head torch all the way through.
It's going to be like an adventure for
me.
As the start gun goes, around 800 skiers
make their way into the Enadin Knight to
tackle the modified 16 km course that
runs for the first time from La Punt
through Samadan to
Pontracina. For many, this is the
adventure of a lifetime.
With their head torches on, the
crosscountry skiers create a magical
atmosphere and supporters and fans cheer
them on their
way. As the hundreds of cross-country
skiers approach the end of the 16
kilometer course, hot refreshments help
them to tackle the last few kilometers.
Reaching the finish area in first place
is 19-year-old Pontracina local Roman
Alder, who is overjoyed with the victory
on his home turf. It's so cool. I'm just
so happy. The finish line is just around
the corner from my house and so it's
exciting. I actually thought that
something might be possible today and I
stayed in the group for a long time, but
to win it's obviously really cool. The
women's category is won by an equally
delighted Yustina Kavalek from Poland.
I'm so happy that I win this race. Uh my
ski was working perfect and I was feel
good working with the men. Uh
I'm happy. I think that I will be sleep
good today.
The day before the main Enadina race,
the marathon village is a hive of
activity. And one of the big draws of
the day is a lecture with four-time
Olympic champion and Swiss cross country
legend Dario Colona, who has a
long-standing connection with the Enadin
Ski
Marathon. I've known the Enadin Ski
Marathon since I was little. I grew up
in Valair which is close to here and
that's how I started here for the first
time when I was 16. I then managed to
win it four times and got second twice.
My first win was definitely special.
That was on my 21st birthday and it was
my first big win internationally. It
gave me a lot of confidence and it was
my breakthrough. That's why that victory
was so
special. The last win was in 2019 and
was also special. The Enadina was part
of the ski Classics and there was a
really strong field and some big
Norwegians. From the stars of old
onwards, it was only five or six of us
and it was a really fast race with a
small group at the front. That I could
win against such strong competition is a
very special memory.
What also makes the Enadina special is
the fact that it's a week-long happening
with 15,000 people here all week. You've
got a lot of side events and it's
important for the sport of crosscountry
skiing that we have such a big race here
in Switzerland.
[Applause]
Another Swiss gear with his own
ambitions is the winner of this year's
night race, 19-year-old Pontracina local
Roman, who hopes that his home field

factor for the Engadina.
We always train on the trails of the
Engin Ski Marathon. We know the course
inside out and have watched the race for
decades. We've seen over and over again
where the important turns and course
parts are and that's what we train for.
Dario Kona is of course a massive legend
and as a small boy it was always
exciting to watch my idol at the Engin
Ski Marathon but now I have races where
I compete against Dario Kona and that's
obviously a lot of fun. I think I
particularly like crosscountry skiing
because you get to know yourself so
well, your body, how far you can push
yourself, what your limits are. It's so
multifaceted, which you possibly
wouldn't experience if you're just
looking from the outside, but you
definitely do when you take part and
experience firsthand the Enadin Ski
It's the morning of the race and as

And even for the season pros like

Now, yeah, I slowly start to get
nervous. The atmosphere builds up and
everyone is here. Soon it all starts.
It's race time and the women's proof
field starts the 54th Endodine Ski
Marathon from the new start location in
Silver
Planner. Shortly after, it's the turn of
the professional men and straight from

Fighting for a good position in the

after, the first amateurs start their
Engin Ski marathon journey. Meanwhile,
up at the front of the women's race,
France's Flora Doli is the early

between herself and the three
chasers. In the men's race, Dario Kona
takes an early lead, but Roman, a couple
of other young Swiss skiers, and the
rest of the pack stay in touching
distance. Soon after, Alder moves past
his idol into first
place. Whilst the professional skiers
are already a few kilometers into the
changed course, more and more amateurs
keep arriving in the busy start area in
Silver Planner. Amongst them is also
Michael Akerman who is in awe of the big
crowds. There are so many people, but
it's well organized and the people are
really friendly. It's
great. Poppy Cross is also getting ready
for her first Enadina and wave after
wave of new groups embark on their
personal Engins.
It's an impressive site as thousands of
crosscountry enthusiasts make their way
onto the modified 40 km course in these
treacherous
conditions. Back at the front, a group
of three skiers, Flora Doli, Enadine
local Juliana Vera and Francis Mayel V
have made a break in the women's field
and are charging ahead full steam.
The men's race is still wide open and
for now the young Swiss skier Fabitio
Albasini manages to maintain his
blistering speed ahead of his teammate
Nicola Viger and a group of four other
skiers. Amongst them their compatriate
Dario Kona and three
Norwegians. Around the 15 kilometer
mark, the six leading men catch up with
the chasing group of the women's pro
race and move past them with
ease. At this point, Albasini and Viger
are still leading the charge, but the
group of six stays tight.
Further back in the field around
Samaritz, the pace isn't quite as
frantic and hundreds of amateurs give it
their best on their personal
Engines. Despite the heavy snowfall, the
support along the course is as strong as
ever. And boyed by the cheering fans,
thousands of skiers make their way
through the Engin's picturesque winter
wonderland.
It's around the 22 km mark in the race
coming off the golf course near Samadan
where the leading six men catch up with
the top three women and spearhead the
race from this point
on. With Norway's Thomas Buer Johansson,
there is now a new leader at the front
of the pack. But this lead group
comprised of three Norwegians and three
Swiss skiers stays tight. In the women's
Flora Doli, Juliana Vera, a male V still
hold their
lead. Even in these challenging
conditions, the trio have found a steady
rhythm, pushing hard on some of the
strenuous hills. 21-year-old male ver
takes control of the race. The French
woman alongside Switzerland's Juliana
Vero now leave the early leader Flora
Doli
behind. As 24year-old Doli struggles to
keep up, the French Swiss duo of Ver and
Vero are now in control of the race.
Back in theata vault, spirits are high
with skiers from around the world
despite battling the tough
conditions. Cheered on by the crowds,
the camaraderie amongst them couldn't be
better. And for all of them, this will
be an unforgettable experience that will
live long in their memories.
Up at the front, the lead has changed
again and Norway's Magna Hager is now
heading up the group of six, followed by
his countrymen Feld Philip Anderson and
Thomas Johansson Bouah. Also still in
the mix, Switzerland's cross-country
legend Dario Kona and the two youngsters
Fabitio Albasini and Nicola Viger who
have left their teammate Roman Alda
trailing in their wake. Suddenly Magna
Hara picks up the pace and moves away
from the five skiers behind
him. Has he landed a decisive blow just
kilometers from the finish line? Hager
keeps up his strong stride as he enters
the finish area in Sump. His compatriate
Thomas Johansen Bua tries everything to
close the
gap, but it's not enough. And the
29-year-old Magna Haga wins his first
ever Engin Ski Marathon ahead of Thomas
Johansen Bua in second place. Shel
Phillip Anderson completes the Norwegian
podium and snatches away third place
from Dario Colonia in the final meters.
I felt like it went from 100 to six
people in just a moment. So that was
kind of good for
us is a special race. So, it's really
fun to have that victory.
After a challenging race, Roman also
reaches the finish line 9 minutes after
the winner in 26th
place. In the women's race, France's
male ver has managed to put a bit of
distance between herself and Juliana
Vero and Italy's Federica Sanfilippo.
The 21-year-old is fighting hard to keep
control. And after a long and tiring
battle, she manages to maintain her lead
all the way to the finish area where she
celebrates her first ever Enadinsky
Marathon victory.
Last year's winner, Juliana Vero of
Switzerland can't quite repeat her 2023
result, but she crosses the finish line
in second place to celebrate her second
consecutive Enadina
podium. Federica Sanfilippo takes third,
but the star of the day is my Elv. I'm
so happy that I've won this race. I
really wasn't expecting too much as
there is a big group of girls, some of
them big names. So yeah, it's lovely
that it's worked out.
As the winners celebrate their Engin Ski
marathon victories, more and more
amateur crosscountry skiers reach the
finish line in Esamp. After more than 4
hours on the 40 km course, a relieved
Michael Akerman also manages to finish
his first ever Enadina. Well, I have to
say it was hard. The wind, the snow, all
of it. I'm really proud.
Throughout the afternoon, skier after
skier crosses the finish line, and every
single one of them celebrates their own
personal victory, that of completing the
54th Engin Ski Marathon.

videos.title: Antoine Rose  - Engadin ski marathon, Saint-Moritz (Switzerland)
videos.description:
The Engadin Skimarathon is an annual cross-country ski race  held on the second Sunday of March in the upper Engadine valley (Switzerland). It is one of the major cross-country skiing events in the Alps. Between 11,000 and 13,000 skiers participate each year. The total distance covered is 42 km. 

Antoine Rose started to shoot the event for the first time in 2014.

videos.title: Norway’s Einar Hedegart Wins 56th Engadin Ski Marathon in Switzerland
videos.description:
In Switzerland’s Upper Engadin valley thousands of athletes gathered for the 56th Engadin Ski marathon one of the world’s premier cross‑country ski races. Spanning 42 kilometers across snowy alpine terrain the event draws both elite competitors and amateur enthusiasts from around the globe.

In a thrilling finish Norway’s Einar Hedegart claimed victory edging out a strong international field in a dramatic sprint. His performance highlighted not only endurance and skill but also the strategic pacing required to conquer the demanding course.

The marathon is more than a race it’s a celebration of winter sport and alpine culture. Skiers navigate classic and freestyle courses braving unpredictable weather and snowy conditions while supporters line the route cheering competitors on.

Organizers emphasize both athletic achievement and environmental stewardship ensuring the event maintains its tradition while protecting the stunning Swiss landscape. For participants crossing the finish line represents not just victory but resilience preparation and the spirit of global camaraderie that defines the Engadin Ski marathon.

#EngadinSkiMarathon #CrossCountrySkiing #WinterSports #Norway #Switzerland

for the 56 Endodine Skiarathon, one of
the world's premier crosscountry ski
races spanning 42 kilometers across
snowy alpine terrain. The event draws
both elite competitors and amateur
enthusiasts from around the globe. In a
thrilling finish, Norway's Anar Hedgart
claimed victory, edging out a strong
international field in a dramatic
sprint. His performance highlighted not
only endurance and skill, but also the
strategic pacing required to conquer the
demanding course. The marathon is more
than a race. It's a celebration of
winter sport and alpine culture. Skiers
navigate classic and freestyle courses,
braving unpredictable weather and snowy
conditions, while supporters line the
route, cheering competitors on.
Organizers emphasize both athletic
achievements and environmental

the stunning Swiss landscape. For
participants, crossing the finish line
represents not just victory, but
resilience, preparation, and the spirit

Angaden Skimmeron. Stay tuned for more

videos.title: 55. Engadin Skimarathon: Rückblick auf die Marathon-Woche
videos.description: Der Engadin Skimarathon ist die grösste Breitensportveranstaltung der Schweiz und der zweitgrösste Langlaufevent weltweit. Seit 1969 laufen Tausende Teilnehmende am zweiten Sonntag im März die Marathonstrecke von Maloja bis S-chanf. Auf dem Programm der Marathonwoche stehen zudem der Engadin Halbmarathon, der Engadin Frauenlauf und der Engadin Nachtlauf.
